transcript.final_response:
Done. I read `/data/export.tsv`, reordered the columns, dropped `id`, and wrote the result to `/data/import.csv`.

**`/data/import.csv`** (comma-separated, with header):

```
email,first_name,last_name,department
alice@co.com,Alice,Smith,Eng
bob@co.com,Bob,Jones,Sales
carol@co.com,Carol,Davis,Eng
dave@co.com,Dave,Wilson,HR
```

Notes:
- Columns are in the requested order: `email, first_name, last_name, department`.
- The `id` column was dropped.
- The original header row was carried through and reordered the same way (since it lined up correctly as the first row, no special handling was needed).
- The input data contained no commas or quotes in any field, so no CSV quoting/escaping was required. If your real data could contain commas, quotes, or newlines inside fields, let me know and I can add proper CSV quoting.
